messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Looking for code for population and sample curve
messages.subject_clean: Looking for code for population and sample curve
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I am looking for code to create normal distribution plots of a population and 5 samples of the population all on one graph.  I can plot all 6 together, but can't seem to adjust the height of the sample plots so that they fit under the population curve.

Thanks,

SC

-- 
Scott R. Colwell, Ph.D.
Associate Professor
University of Guelph
College of Management and Economics
Guelph, Ontario, Canada, N1G 2W1

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Looking for code for population and sample curve
messages.subject_clean: Looking for code for population and sample curve
messages.body_plain:
What types of plots are you trying to get?  Qq-plots? Histograms of samples compared to a line showing the population?  Density estimates?

-- 
Gregory (Greg) L. Snow Ph.D.
Statistical Data Center
Intermountain Healthcare
greg.snow at imail.org
801.408.8111

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Short courses in statistics
messages.body_plain:
Dear friends,

INSEED is happy to announce two short courses, viz.

* 10-14 November, 2010: Statistical methods in Epidemiology (using R), 
Anand, Gujarat

* 22-26 November, 2010: Bayesian computation using OpenBUGS (and R), 
Vellore, Tamil Nadu

The details of the workshop and the registration procedure are at 
http://www.inseed.org/workshops/.

Bijoy Joseph
Course coordinator
____________________________________________________
Indic Society for Education and Development (INSEED)
1, Swami Enterprises complex,
Tigrania Road, Nashik - 422011, India.

Tel: +91-9890729200, +91-253-2503969
http://www.inseed.org/

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] question
messages.body_plain:
Dear solafah

This is a list for discussion of use of R in teaching.

Your question would be more appropriate for the r-help list, assuming
it's not a homework assignment.  It is an amusing question though.
One way would be to make use of the inverse CDF method, combined with
uniformly sampling from some interval like (0, .05) or (.95,1).  I'll
leave the details as an exercise for the r student.

albyn

On Thu, Aug 19, 2010 at 04:33:34PM -0700, solafah bh wrote:
> Hello
> I want to know how can i sampling from upper and lower tail of normal distribution, by R code?
> ?
> Regards
> 
> 
>       
> 	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
> 

> _______________________________________________
> R-sig-teaching at r-project.org mailing list
> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-teaching


-- 
Albyn Jones
Reed College
jones at reed.edu

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Entering Data into R and running a bootstrap
messages.subject_clean: Entering Data into R and running a bootstrap
messages.body_plain:
On Fri, 2010-08-20 at 10:31 +0000, Yemitan Raphael wrote:
> Hello,
>  
> I want to know how to enter data into R, how to run a bootstrap
> regression and also how to test for serial correlation.

Wrong list. Try R-Help, but not before you've read the posting guide as
you won't get an answer to your question if you send the same message
there.

G

--
